page: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with x86_64 architecture, offering 48 virtual CPUs (24 physical cores with hyper-threading) running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz. The CPU includes a 35 MB L3 cache and supports instruction sets such as AVX, AVX-512F, AES, and FMA. The instance is equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks position the m5.12xlarge in the middle tier for most multi-core and memory operations. While single-core performance, such as stress-ng single-core and single-threaded gzip compression, is categorized as weak, its multi-core capabilities are robust. The instance achieves average ratings in multi-core stress-ng tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and Passmark CPU tests. Memory bandwidth for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks remains within the average range. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also demonstrate stable, mid-tier performance. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference speeds for small and medium models are average, though prompt processing for large 70B models drops to the bottom 25%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5.12xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for enterprise workloads that require substantial memory and multi-core processing without the premium cost of specialized compute or memory-optimized hardware. The baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps supports reliable data transfer. This instance is well-suited for general-purpose workloads, including multi-threaded application servers, medium-sized databases, static web serving, software compilation, and moderate data processing tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor, featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs (24 physical cores) running at 3.1 GHz and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on network-attached storage and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU and memory performance, though single-core execution speeds are relatively weak. The instance delivers stable, mid-tier throughput for database operations, Redis SET tasks, and static web serving. Qualitatively, it provides a balanced resource-to-cost profile for workloads requiring high core counts and memory capacity without specialized hardware. It is best suited for multi-threaded application servers, software compilation, and general-purpose enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 192 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor, delivering balanced multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5.12xlarge is a general-purpose Nitro instance with 48 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and average multi-core performance.
tagline: A balanced x86 general-purpose instance offering 48 vCPUs and 192 GB RAM for multi-threaded enterprise workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: Powered by a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores.
Equipped with 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a balanced 4:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio.
Delivers 12 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU accelerators.
Demonstrates average multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database performance in benchmarks.
Suited for multi-threaded application servers, software compilation, and general-purpose workloads.
